---
title: "Step 4 — HEX data"
---

> ⚠ **This step depends on the hex data access pipeline, which is under active
> development.** The design is specified in
> [arch-11](https://github.com/worldbank/devPTIpack/blob/main/.github/docs/arch-11-hex-data-access.md).
> Functions described below do not yet exist in the package — this page
> documents the intended workflow.

## What HEX data is

Pre-computed indicator values stored at H3 hexagonal resolution 6 (~36 km² per
cell) in online parquet files maintained by the project team. Each parquet
contains one or more indicator columns keyed by H3 hex index, with an optional
time dimension (one row per hex × year).

The hex data pipeline lets you:

1. **Browse** available indicators via a package-shipped registry.
2. **Fetch** values for only the hexagons covering your country's boundaries.
3. **Aggregate** hex values to your admin polygons using configurable
   weighting and aggregation strategies.
4. **Produce** a standard `metadata-hex.xlsx` that Step 5's
   `compile_pti_data()` merges with your user metadata.

Population is always fetched automatically from the registry and is used for
population-weighted aggregation. It also appears as a visible indicator in the
deployed app.

## Prerequisites

Step 1 must be complete. You need:

- `app-data/shapes.rds` containing your admin layers **and** an
  `admin9_Hexagon` layer built by `make_hex_grid()` and enriched by
  `make_admin_lookup()`.

```r
my_shp    <- readRDS("app-data/shapes.rds")
hex_layer <- my_shp$admin9_Hexagon
hex_ids   <- hex_layer$admin9Pcod
```

## Discovering available variables

```r
list_hex_vars()
```

Returns a tibble — one row per registered variable — showing canonical name,
display name, units, pillar, whether it has a time dimension, available years
(hint from registry), and default aggregation strategy.

Filter by pillar or temporal status:

```r
list_hex_vars(pillar = "Economic Activity")
list_hex_vars(temporal = TRUE)
```

To query the **live** parquet for actual available years (not just the registry
hint):

```r
get_available_years(vars = NULL, "nightlights")
#> nightlights (PTI Pre-computed Hex Data): 2013 2014 2015 ... 2022
```

## Selecting variables and years

```r
vars <- use_hex_vars(
  "nightlights",
  "poverty_rate",
  "population",
  years = c(2015, 2022)
)
```

`years` applies globally to all temporal variables. Non-temporal variables
(like a static poverty estimate) are returned regardless.

**Year resolution rules:**

- Exact year available → used as-is.
- Exact year absent → nearest available year substituted. On equidistant
  tie, the **later** year is preferred. A warning is emitted.
- No data within 7 years of a requested year → error with available years
  listed.

In an interactive session with `years = NULL`, the system prints available
years and prompts you to choose. In a non-interactive session (e.g.
`quarto render`), `years = NULL` errors with an actionable message.

## Fetching hex data

```r
hex_data <- fetch_hex_data(hex_ids = hex_ids, vars = vars)
```

Only hexagons matching your `hex_ids` are read (parquet predicate pushdown).
Temporal variables are pivoted wide: `nightlights_2015`, `nightlights_2022`.
Population is always the first indicator column after `hex_id`.

**Resolution bridge:** if your hex grid is at H5 (coarser than the H6 source
data), `fetch_hex_data()` transparently expands each H5 cell to its H6
children, fetches at H6, and aggregates back to H5. Requesting finer than H6
(e.g. H7) produces an error.

Progress is reported per variable.

## Optional: merging your own data

If you have project-specific indicators in a local parquet file, merge them
between fetch and aggregate:

```r
my_data <- arrow::open_dataset("data-raw/conflict.parquet") |>
  dplyr::filter(h3id %in% hex_ids) |>
  dplyr::collect() |>
  dplyr::rename(hex_id = h3id) |>
  tidyr::pivot_wider(
    names_from  = year,
    values_from = conflict_events,
    names_prefix = "conflict_"
  )

hex_data <- dplyr::full_join(hex_data, my_data, by = "hex_id")
```

**Local parquet contract:**

| Requirement | Rule |
|---|---|
| Format | Readable by `arrow::open_dataset()` |
| Layout | Long format — one row per hex × time period |
| Hex column | Any name — you rename to `hex_id` before merge |
| Population | Absent (comes from registry) |
| Values | Numeric columns, one per indicator |
| Uniqueness | No duplicate hex ID per time period |

## Aggregating to admin polygons

```r
aggregated <- aggregate_hex_to_shapes(
  hex_data  = hex_data,
  hex_layer = hex_layer,
  shp_dta   = my_shp,
  strategy  = list(
    .default    = c(weight = "pop",  fun = "mean"),
    nightlights = c(weight = "none", fun = "mean"),
    population  = c(weight = "none", fun = "sum"),
    conflict    = c(weight = "none", fun = "sum")
  )
)
```

**Strategy has two orthogonal dimensions:**

- **weight**: `"pop"` (population-weighted), `"area"` (area-weighted), or
  `"none"` (unweighted / plutocratic).
- **fun**: `"mean"`, `"median"`, `"sum"`, `"min"`, `"max"`.

A `.default` entry covers all variables not explicitly listed. For temporal
variables, use the **stem** name (e.g. `nightlights` covers both
`nightlights_2015` and `nightlights_2022`).

Aggregation is always **hex → each admin level directly** (hex→admin1,
hex→admin2, etc.) — never chained through intermediate levels.

`NA` values are treated as 0. If all hexes in a polygon are `NA` for a
variable, the result is `NA` with a warning.

## Building the metadata Excel

```r
build_hex_metadata(
  aggregated       = aggregated,
  shp_dta          = my_shp,
  indicator_config = tibble::tibble(
    canonical_name        = "conflict",
    var_name              = "Conflict Events ({year})",
    var_description       = "ACLED conflict event count.",
    var_units             = "count",
    pillar_group          = 4,
    pillar_name           = "Security",
    legend_revert_colours = TRUE,
    fltr_exclude_pti      = FALSE
  ),
  country_name = "Rwanda",
  output_path  = "app-data/metadata-hex.xlsx",
  include_hex  = INCLUDE_HEX_IN_APP
)
```

Registry variables have their metadata auto-populated from the YAML — you only
need `indicator_config` for non-registry variables (your local parquet
indicators). You can override any registry default via `indicator_config`.

Temporal variables expand to one metadata row per resolved year, with
`var_name` generated from the glue template (e.g. `"Night Lights (2015)"`).

### The `INCLUDE_HEX_IN_APP` switch

Set in `00-master.R`:

```r
INCLUDE_HEX_IN_APP <- FALSE
```

| Value | Behaviour |
|---|---|
| `TRUE` | Hex-level `admin9_Hexagon` sheet included in metadata Excel. Hex polygons shipped with the app. |
| `FALSE` (default) | Hex sheet omitted. Only aggregated admin-level sheets written. All hex-sourced indicator values still available at admin levels. |

When `include_hex` is not set and the hex grid exceeds 5,000 cells, the system
warns and prompts interactively (defaults to `FALSE` in non-interactive
sessions).

## Supported admin levels

Hex-derived indicators appear on **every** admin-level sheet in the output
Excel (admin1, admin2, etc.). `spatial_level` in the metadata sheet points to
the **hex level** (e.g. `admin9_Hexagon`) — the finest available level.

## Note on ordering

This step is **order-flexible** — it can run before or after Step 3. The two
outputs (`metadata-user.xlsx` and `metadata-hex.xlsx`) are independent
intermediate files merged by Step 5's `compile_pti_data()`.

## Next

Once `app-data/metadata-hex.xlsx` is produced, continue with
[Step 5 — Compile & finalise](build-pti-5-compile.html).
